One-Handed.wikitext:
[[File:Onehandedlogo.png|thumb|200x200px]]
==General Information==
One-Handed weapons are one of the five weapon classes. Their Attack Speed is the second slowest of the different weapon types, and their Attack stat is the second highest when compared to other weapon types of equal Skill Level rating.
*Contrary to player belief, having a Shield equipped will not affect your attack speed.
*Critical Chance of all Weapon Types is 15%, raising LUK up to max will add up to +5% Critical Chance, totaling 20% with 500 LUK invested.
*Critical Hit damage is calculated as (base*crit multi) + (base*STR multi)
** "STR Multi" ranges from 0 - 2 based off your STR points invested.
**Critical Hits with One-Handed weapons will multiply your Base Attack stat by 2.

{{FrontPage Header|Skills|color = {{Themecolor}}}}
{{FrontPage TableStart}}
This weapon type currently has 7 unlockable sword skills. Reaver is a Universal sword skill that all weapons will unlock first at 5 Skill. Each new sword skill unlocked is stronger than the previous one, and capable of hitting more enemies at the same time. Investing points into AGI will increase your weapon's Attack Speed, the speed of the 4 lower level skills, and lower Sword Skill cooldowns.

===Dual Blades===
[[File:Dual Blades.png|right|150x150px]]
Dual Blades is a Passive Skill that is unlockable by acquiring 200 One-Handed Skill. The option to equip a Second One-Handed sword will appear in your Equipment menu, marked with a "-" symbol. Gale Slicer, Starburst, and Starburst Stream require Dual Blades to use them.

When using Dual Blades, both swords connect to give damage separately and have their own independent cooldown interval.  Your damage is doubled in this manner, and your Attack Speed can be increased by investing into AGI.

Rapier.wikitext:
[[File:Rapierlogo.png|thumb|200x200px]]
==General Information==
Rapiers are one of the five weapon classes. Their Attack Speed is in the middle of the different weapon types, and their Attack stat is the same when compared to other weapon types of equal Skill Level rating. 
* Contrary to player belief, having a Shield equipped will not affect your attack speed.
*Critical Chance of all Weapon Types is 15%, raising LUK up to max will add up to +5% Critical Chance, totaling 20% with 500 LUK invested.
*Critical Hit damage is calculated as (base*crit multi) + (base*STR multi)
**"STR Multi" ranges from 0 - 2 based off your STR points invested.
**Critical Hits with Rapiers will multiply your Base Attack stat by 2.4.

{{FrontPage Header|Skills|color = {{Themecolor}}}}
{{FrontPage TableStart}}
This weapon type currently has 7 unlockable sword skills. Reaver is a Universal sword skill that all weapons will unlock first at 5 Skill. Each new sword skill unlocked is stronger than the previous one, and capable of hitting more enemies at the same time. Investing points into AGI will increase your weapon's Attack Speed, the speed of the 4 lower level skills, and lower Sword Skill cooldowns.

Dagger.wikitext:
[[File:Daggerlogo.png|thumb|200x200px]]
==General Information==
Daggers are one of the five weapon classes. Their Attack Speed is the second fastest of the different weapon types, and their Attack stat is the second lowest when compared to other weapon types of equal Skill Level rating.
*Contrary to player belief, having a Shield equipped will not affect your attack speed.
*Critical Chance of all Weapon Types is 15%, raising LUK up to max will add up to +5% Critical Chance, totaling 20% with 500 LUK invested.
*Critical Hit damage is calculated as (base*crit multi) + (base*STR multi)
**"STR Multi" ranges from 0 - 2 based off your STR points invested.
**Critical Hits with Daggers will multiply your weapon's Base Attack stat by 2.7.

{{FrontPage Header|Skills|color = {{Themecolor}}}}
{{FrontPage TableStart}}
This weapon type currently has 7 unlockable sword skills. Reaver is a Universal sword skill that all weapons will unlock first at 5 Skill. Each new sword skill unlocked is stronger than the previous one, and capable of hitting more enemies at the same time. Investing points into AGI will increase your weapon's Attack Speed, the speed of the 4 lower level skills, and lower Sword Skill cooldowns.

Armor.wikitext:
[[File:Armorlogo.png|thumb|200x200px]]
==General Information==
Armor is essential to survive in this game, whether it is a mob, boss, or Player you're fighting. As you keep leveling up, you'll be able to equip better armor to survive. Armor provides Defense and Dexterity, with Defense reducing the damage you receive, and Dexterity granting you more Health.

Defense and Dexterity stat calculations are as follows:
*Defense Stat x 5 = Flat damage reduction.
*A total of 300.5 Defense from your equipment subtracts Damage received from hits by 1,502.5.
**Each stat point invested in DEF adds 0.01 to the multiplier. With 500 DEF points, the calculation will be Defense Stat x 10.
**A total of 300.5 Defense from your equipment, with 500 DEF points invested, subtracts Damage received from hits by 3005.
*Dexterity Stat x 10 = Bonus Health from equipment.
*A total of 2,402 Dexterity from your equipment will grant +24,020 Health. 
**Each stat point invested in VIT adds 0.01 to the multiplier. With 500 VIT points, the calculation will be Dexterity Stat x 15.
**A total of 2,402 Dexterity from your equipment, with 500 VIT points invested, will grant a total of +36,030 Health.
{{FrontPage Header|List of Armor|color = {{Themecolor}}}}
{{FrontPage TableStart}}
*Any armor with a Resistance stat provides 40% Resistance, which lowers the amount of time you're inflicted with the corresponding status effect, and lowers any damage that you receive from it.
**Each stat point invested in VIT adds +0.01% Resistance to All status effects. With 500 VIT points, you will have +5% Resistance to All status effects.
*Any damage from Status Effects will show Purple damage numbers.
*Any damage from Sword Skills will show Orange damage numbers.

Please note that armor for each associated floor will have their Level rated according to how they are obtained.
*Crafted - Highest
*Boss Drop - 2nd Highest
*Mob Drop - 2nd Lowest
*Shops - Lowest

Badge and Gamepass Armor will scale according to your Level: 
*Badge Armor will scale up every 5 Levels.
*Gamepass Armor will scale up every Level.
*Any Armor with a Max Level stat ingame will have both their Max Level and Max Stats displayed in the table below.

Please click on the name of the Armor to see a full page of their stats.

Shields.wikitext:
[[File:Shield.png|thumb|200x200px]]
== General Information ==
Shields act as additional defense on top of your armor and are highly recommended to use if you're using 1 One Handed Sword, a Rapier, or a Dagger. Neglecting the option of using a Shield will hurt your survivability on higher floors as mobs get increasingly tougher.

*Damage Reduction is calculated as (defense*5).
**Having a total of 400 Defense from your Equipment will subtract 2000 damage from all enemy attacks.
*Investing DEF Points will raise the multiplier from 5 - 10, depending on the amount of DEF points invested.
*A maximum of 500 DEF points invested will result in your Damage Reduction calculated as (defense*10).
**With the maximum of 500 DEF points invested, having a total of 400 Defense from your Equipment will subtract 4000 damage from all enemy attacks.

Upper Headwear.wikitext:
[[File:Headwearlogo.png|thumb|200x200px]]
Upper Headwear provides supplemental Health to aid in survival of this game. They mostly consist of Helmets, Hats, and Crowns.

Defense and Dexterity stat calculations are as follows:
*Defense Stat x 5 = Flat damage reduction.
*A total of 300.5 Defense from your equipment subtracts Damage received from hits by 1,502.5.
**Each stat point invested in DEF adds 0.01 to the multiplier. With 500 DEF points, the calculation will be Defense Stat x 10.
**A total of 300.5 Defense from your equipment, with 500 DEF points invested, subtracts Damage received from hits by 3005.
*Dexterity Stat x 10 = Bonus Health from equipment.
*A total of 2,402 Dexterity from your equipment will grant +24,020 Health. 
**Each stat point invested in VIT adds 0.01 to the multiplier. With 500 VIT points, the calculation will be Dexterity Stat x 15.
**A total of 2,402 Dexterity from your equipment, with 500 VIT points invested, will grant a total of +36,030 Health.

{{FrontPage Header|List of Upper Headwear|color = {{Themecolor}}}}
{{FrontPage TableStart}}
*Any Upper Headwear with a Resistance stat provides 20% Resistance, which lowers the amount of time you're inflicted with the corresponding status effect, and lowers any damage that you receive from it.
**Each stat point invested in VIT adds +0.01% Resistance to All status effects. With 500 VIT points, you will have +5% Resistance to All status effects.
*Any damage from Status Effects will show Purple damage numbers.
*Any damage from Sword Skills will show Orange damage numbers.

Please note that Headwear for each associated floor will have their Level rated according to how they are obtained.
*Crafted - Highest
*Boss Drop - 2nd Highest
*Mob Drop - 2nd Lowest
*Shops - Lowest

Badge and Gamepass Headwear will scale according to your Level: 
*Badge Headwear will scale up every 5 Levels.
*Gamepass Headwear will scale up every Level.
*Any Headwear with a Max Level stat ingame will have both their Max Level and Max Stats displayed in the table below.

[[File:GamepassBadgeMerchant.png|thumb|250x250px|'''Gamepass and Badge Merchant in Floor 1 Shop''']] ==Gamepass Equipment==
Gamepass Equipment are special equipment that will scale to your corresponding Level or Skill, getting stronger as you continue leveling up until they reach their Max Level or Max Skill stat. ==Badge Equipment ==
[[File:OverlayMerchant.png|thumb|250x250px|'''Overlay Merchant in Floor 1 Shop''']] Badge Equipment are special equipment that will scale to your corresponding Level or Skill, getting stronger as you continue leveling up until they reach their Max Level or Max Skill stat. Inspecting the weapon's stats ingame will allow you to check the Max Stat that can be reached and the stats they currently have at the moment. ===How Scaling Works=== *Badge Armor, Shields, Upper Headwear, and Lower Headwear will scale up every 5 Levels. *'''EX''': If you are Level 12, your Festive Kimono will have their stats set to Level 10. [[Category:Miscellaneous]]

Stats.wikitext: Stats are a broad term in SBO:R, because basically everything is a statistic. On this page we will be going through important non-investible stats and all of the investible stats, along with some other stats which don't really fit in either section. == Non-Investible Stats == === '''Attack''' === Attack is a stat found on all [[:Category:Weapons|Weapon Types]] in the game, and directly correlate to how much damage they deal. 1 Attack stat on a weapon is 1 damage dealt with the weapon, not taking into account STR. === '''Defense''' === Defense is a stat found on [[Armor]], [[Shields]] and some rare [[Upper Headwear]]. Every point of defense is a 5 damage reduction on any non-status effect damage taken (capped at a minimum of 5), not taking into account DEF. Total damage reduction without DEF is calculated with the formula: 5 * '''Total Defense'''. === '''Dexterity''' === Dexterity is a stat found on [[Armor]], [[Upper Headwear]] and [[Lower Headwear]] that determines your Maximum HP, a HUD stat determined by the both your equipment and Levels. Every point of dexterity gives 10 max HP to your total, not taking into account VIT. The formula for Max hp increase without VIT is: 10 * '''Total Dexterity'''. === '''Stamina''' === Stamina is a stat not found on equipment, but it is a HUD stat increased through leveling up that is consumed while sprinting, jumping, blocking and attacking (different weapon types consume different amounts of Stamina). The formula for calculating Stamina (without the affects of AGI, STR or VIT) is 100 + ('''Player Level''' * 5). If a player were to max out STR, AGI and VIT (more info on them down below), the formula would be 250 + ('''Player Level''' * 5). === '''Multi-Hits''' === Multi-hits are a hidden statistic that each weapon type has access to when reaching 200+ Sword Skill, with two exceptions. The first being Dual Wielding, which has two individual hitboxes with two different swords, basically achieving its own form of Multi-hit. The second being 2 Handed weapons, which has a chance Multi-hit as soon as you start to use it. Multi-hits allow you to sometimes hit multiple times with one swing of your weapon, either on one target twice or two targets once, usually making it easier to deal with groups. Without STR or LUK, the base chance to Multi-hit enemies is 50%. Multi-hits can never hit more then two times for normal attacks, be it on separate enemies or the same enemies. == Investible Stats == === STR === ==== '''Stat Changes''' ==== Every point of STR invested is a 0.4% increase of damage, totaling out at a complete +200% damage at 500 points atop of your base damage, becoming a 3x multiplier of your base damage. Along with the damage multiplier, every point in STR increases your base Stamina by 0.1, giving you a +50 to base stamina at 500 points invested. ==== '''Combat Changes''' ==== With every point invested in STR, the chance for Multi-hitting enemies increases by 0.02%, capping out at a 10% increase (Total increase cannot exceed 15% if LUK is included as well). In combination with LUK, you can attain a total 15% increase to your Multi-hit chance, totaling out at a 65% chance to Multi-hit. === DEF === Every point of DEF invested adds 0.01 to your base defense multiplier, adding in an additional +5 to your base multiplier at 500 Points. With 500 DEF, the formula for damage reduction would be: 10 * '''Total Defense''', meaning every point of Defense is worth 10 points of reduced damage. === AGI === AGI has the largest amount of different increases per stat out of any of the investible stats, so they will be listed in short hand then explained individually. The changes per point are; +0.1 stamina, +0.004 stud/s walk speed, +0.02 stud/s sprint speed, approximatly -0.001s jump interval, reduced attack intervals for each weapon type. ==== '''Stat Changes'''==== With every point invested in AGI, your base Stamina increases by 0.1, giving you a +50 to base stamina at 500 points invested. ===='''Movement Changes'''==== With every point in AGI, your base walk and run speeds increase. Walk speed increases by 0.004 Studs/s and Run speed increases by 0.02 Studs/s per point, maxing out at +2 Studs/s walk speed and +10 Studs/s Run speed at 500 points. Along with increase movement speeds, jump time intervals also get reduced with your agility level allowing you to jump more frequently. Every point in AGI gives approximatly -0.001s jump delay (Approximatly because the base jump interval isnt exactaly 1 second). This means with Max AGI, you can push the jump time interval all the way down to approximately 0.5 seconds. ===='''Combat Changes'''==== Damage attack intervals (Swinging speed) for each weapon type gets changed as AGI increases. Because of the wildly different nature and swing speeds of each weapon type, we will only be comparing their base speed and max AGI speed. In addition, the changes in speed will be given as approximate percentages. * '''1 Handed''' weapons just under 2.5 seconds to swing three times at base speed, and at max AGI it takes just over 1.75 seconds. This means there is approximately a 30% increase of speed between base and max. * '''Dual Wielding''' straight up has its delay cut in half, going from Base speed of just under 1 second to a Max speed of just under 0.5 seconds, so there is a 50% increase of speed. * '''2 Handed''' weapons at base speed take 3.5 seconds to swing 3 times, and at max speed it takes 2.5 seconds to swing 3 times. This gives us an approximate speed increase just under 30%. * '''Rapiers''' at base speed take 1.75 seconds to swing 3 times, and at max speed they take 0.66 seconds to swing 3 times. This gives the largest speed increase out of all the weapons an approximate 60%. * '''Daggers''' at base speed take 1.25 seconds to swing 3 times, and at max speed they take just under 1 second to swing 3 times. This means the approximate speed increase is just over 30%. ===VIT=== Every point of VIT invested increases the multiplier of the Dexterity by 0.01, maxing out at a +5 to the multiplier at 500 points invested. This means when maxed, the Dexterity equation for Max HP is: 15 * '''Total Dexterity'''. In addition to the Dexterity multiplier, every point invested gives +0.1 base stamina, maxing out at +50 with 500 points. Finally, with each point invested the player gains +0.01% resistance to all debuffs, which caps out at 5% at 500 points. This allows the player to get resistance to debuffs that don't have armor dedicated to them such as On Fire. ===LUK === ==== '''Stat Changes'''==== LUK increases two different things every time you invest a point in it: * Every point invested equates +0.01% chance towards critical hits, capping out at +5% when at 500 invested points. * Every point invested gives +0.01% chance for items to drop, capping out at +5% at 500 invested points. In addition to the +5%, when you get to 500 points, every time an item doesn't drop, you get an additional drop chance with double the probability from beforehand. The Formula for this extra drop chance is (Base Chance x 2) +5%. For example, say something had a base 5% drop chance, and you didn't get it on the initial drop (10% Chance), the 500 point drop chance would have a 15% chance of giving you the item. This chance only occurs if you did '''not''' get the item on the first drop chance. ==== '''Combat Changes''' ==== With every point invested in LUK, the chance for Multi-hitting enemies increases by 0.02%, capping out at a 10% increase (Total increase cannot exceed 15% if STR is included aswell). In combination with STR, you can attain a total 15% increase to your Multi-hit chance, totalling out at a 65% chance to Multi-hit. == Miscellaneous Stats== ===Resistances === Resistances are stats that appear on [[Armor]], [[Upper Headwear]] and [[Lower Headwear]], along with steadily increasing with the VIT stat as points are invested in it, Resistances work in percentages, every item gives a different % resistance to a status effect. Armour gives 40% resistance to whatever debuff it lists, Upper and Lower Headwear gives 20% respectively, and each point in VIT gives 0.01% resistance to all debuffs (Maximising at 5%). This means the maximum resistance you can get to a single debuff in SBO:R is 85%. Each debuff has one of two ways its affected by a resistance, either its duration is reduced, or its damage is reduced. [[Status Effects#Frozen|Frozen]] and [[Status Effects#Paralysis|Paralysis]] have their duration reduced by the % of resistance they have, and all the other status effects have their damage reduced by the % of resistance. The exception to this is the Light debuff, which only has positive effects (for players), meaning no resistance affects it. [[Category:Info]]
